analysis.name: Existence of objective truth
analysis.statement: Objective truth exists independently of any particular observer, government, or culture.
analysis.necessity_reason: The theory uses deductive inference throughout. Without Existence of objective truth, the basic inferential structure of the theory collapses.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY. It should be elevated to the named axiom set for formal completeness.

analysis.name: Reliability of human reason
analysis.statement: Human reason, when applied correctly to consistent formal systems, produces reliable conclusions.
analysis.necessity_reason: The theory uses deductive inference throughout. Without Reliability of human reason, the basic inferential structure of the theory collapses.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY. It should be elevated to the named axiom set for formal completeness.

analysis.name: Causal structure of the world
analysis.statement: The world has a stable causal structure such that if A causes B in one case, A will cause B in relevantly similar cases.
analysis.necessity_reason: Causal structure of the world is required for game-theoretic and property-persistence claims. Without it, the temporal stability proofs fail.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY_FOR_STABILITY_ARGUMENTS. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Persistence of personal identity
analysis.statement: A human being is the same person across time; the one who makes a contract is the same one who fulfills it.
analysis.necessity_reason: Persistence of personal identity is required for game-theoretic and property-persistence claims. Without it, the temporal stability proofs fail.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY_FOR_STABILITY_ARGUMENTS. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Non-arbitrariness of logical inference
analysis.statement: Valid deductive inference is not arbitrary: if premises are true, the conclusion cannot be false.
analysis.necessity_reason: The theory uses deductive inference throughout. Without Non-arbitrariness of logical inference, the basic inferential structure of the theory collapses.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY. It should be elevated to the named axiom set for formal completeness.

analysis.name: Uniqueness of the formal system for liberty
analysis.statement: There exists at most one consistent formal axiomatic system that correctly captures individual liberty and property rights.
analysis.necessity_reason: Uniqueness of the formal system for liberty is required for the AI-ethics uniqueness conclusion. Without it the theory offers A solution, not THE solution.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is LOAD_BEARING_FOR_TERMINAL_CLAIMS. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Interpretive continuity of religious texts
analysis.statement: The Qur'an, Hadith, and the sayings of the Ahl al-Bayt can be read as a consistent, coherent system across their historical transmission.
analysis.necessity_reason: Interpretive continuity of religious texts is necessary only if the theory claims theological sources are consistent and authoritative. For the purely secular kernel, optional.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is CONDITIONALLY_NECESSARY. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Transferability of Gödel's theorems to social systems
analysis.statement: Gödel's incompleteness theorems, proved for formal arithmetic, apply meaningfully to axiomatic systems governing human social organization.
analysis.necessity_reason: Transferability of Gödel's theorems to social systems is directly load-bearing for the CFS methodology. If Gödel's theorems do not transfer, the capital-C 'Consistent' language collapses.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is CRITICAL. It should be elevated to the named axiom set for formal completeness.

analysis.name: Inviolability of the body as property
analysis.statement: The human body is not only property but is inviolable property — no contract can legitimately transfer total ownership of one's body to another.
analysis.necessity_reason: Inviolability of the body as property is required to derive deontic claims (obligations/prohibitions). Without it the theory can describe property but not prescribe its protection.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY_FOR_DEONTIC_LAYER. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Commensurability of liberty across individuals
analysis.statement: Different persons' claims to liberty are commensurable — when two claims conflict, a principled resolution exists.
analysis.necessity_reason: Commensurability of liberty across individuals is required to derive deontic claims (obligations/prohibitions). Without it the theory can describe property but not prescribe its protection.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY_FOR_DEONTIC_LAYER. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Minimal distinguishability of God
analysis.statement: God is minimally distinguishable from all observable entities — the definition 'God is not any of the things we see' is sufficient to produce agreement-admissible propositions.
analysis.necessity_reason: Minimal distinguishability of God is necessary only if the theory claims theological sources are consistent and authoritative. For the purely secular kernel, optional.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is CONDITIONALLY_NECESSARY. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

analysis.name: Moral realism
analysis.statement: Moral facts (e.g., 'slavery is wrong') are objective and discoverable by reason, not merely conventional or preference-based.
analysis.necessity_reason: Moral realism (moral realism) is required for moral claims to have binding force beyond mere preference. Without it, the theory is descriptive only.
analysis.auditor_note: This axiom is NECESSARY_FOR_NORMATIVE_FORCE. It need not be in the named axiom set but should be acknowledged as a pre-condition.

key_finding: 3 hidden axioms (HA-001, HA-002, HA-005) are secretly foundational: objective truth, reliable reason, and valid inference are prerequisites for every step of every derivation. They should be elevated to the named axiom set. Without them, the theory's entire deductive framework rests on unacknowledged presuppositions. HA-008 (Gödel transfer) is CRITICAL: it is load-bearing for the CFS methodology but unproven. HA-006 (uniqueness) is the most consequential for the terminal claims.
